sanskrit:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये।
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम्॥
transliteration:
jarā-maraṇa-mokṣāya mām āśritya yatanti ye
te brahma tad viduḥ kṛtsnam adhyātmaṃ karma cākhilam
literal_meaning: Those who strive for liberation from old-age-and-death, taking refuge in Me, know Brahman completely, the Self, and all action.
contextual_meaning: When a person faces existential anxiety (aging, mortality) and chooses a secure attachment to the Divine rather than to finite objects, cognitive clarity dawns: they experientially grasp the whole field of consciousness (Brahman), the inner observer (Self), and the mechanics of action without being trapped by it.
psychological_state: Secure existential base; low death-anxiety; meta-cognitive awareness that observes thoughts/actions without fusion; identity shifts from body-time to witnessing awareness.
trigger_scenario: A 50-year-old receives a health-scare diagnosis; instead of frantic Googling or total avoidance, she joins a daily meditation/kirtan group, finds steady presence, and reports, 'I still see the numbers on the lab report, but they no longer define who I am.'
anchor_text: Cling to the Divine and death’s grip loosens, revealing the eternal Self.
